concerns.text: Phase 6 (Data Acquisition) tasks T036-T039 are listed AFTER User Stories 1-3 (Phases 3-5). However, the 'Dependencies & Execution Order' section explicitly states US1 and US2 'Depends on Phase 6 (Data) to have a valid stream.' A consumer (US1/US2) cannot logically precede its producer (Data Acquisition). Phase 6 must be moved to Phase 2 (Foundational) or immediately after Phase 2 to satisfy the 'Producer before consumer' rule.

Task T034 (Sensitivity Analysis) re-runs the training loop with multiple seeds. However, there is no preceding task ensuring the *input data* (features and targets) is regenerated or re-sampled deterministically for each seed. If the data stream or feature extraction is stochastic, re-running only the training loop (T034) violates the reproducibility requirement (Constitution Principle I) and creates a hidden dependency on the initial data run. A task to re-generate/re-sample data per seed must precede T034.

Test tasks T009-T011 are listed before implementation tasks T012-T017. While the 'Implementation Strategy' mentions 'Verify tests fail before implementing,' the task list order implies tests are executed before the code exists. For a deterministic execution plan, implementation tasks (T012-T017) should precede their corresponding test tasks, or the test tasks should be explicitly marked as 'Write test scaffolding' rather than 'Run unit test' to avoid logical impossibility in the first pass.
